seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between St Johns Park and St Leonards. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: St Johns Park vs St Leonards: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Johns Park and St Leonards.
heading: St Johns Park vs St Leonards — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Johns Park, New South Wales and St Leonards, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Leonards has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to St Johns Park (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Johns Park or St Leonards safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Johns Park has a flood risk score of 46/100, while St Leonards scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Johns Park scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. St Leonards scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Johns Park or St Leonards?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Leonards has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
